# {{Group Name}}

<!-- Groups are the optional middle level in the project hierarchy
     (Project → Group → Component → Code; see docs/ARCHITECTURE.md §4).
     They organize a coherent sub-system within a project, and document
     what crosses their children's boundaries.

     Groups own NO code directly — that's the children's job. Groups
     own integration / orchestration / shared contracts / shared schemas.

     Sections are classified by authorship:
       HUMAN        — written by the human; never overwritten by /consolidate.
       AUTO         — regenerated by /consolidate from current state on each pass.
       APPEND-AUTO  — /consolidate appends new entries; existing rows preserved.

     Frontmatter notes:
       parent  — either the project name (for top-level groups) or another
                 group name (for sub-groups; recursive).
       contains — children: components (specs / workflows) and/or sub-groups.
                  Bidirectional ownership: each child declares
                  `parent: {{this-group}}`. -->

## Job (JTBD)

<!-- HUMAN — Required. What is this sub-system hired to do as a coherent unit?
     Bridges the project-level JTBD and the children's individual JTBDs.

     Example (data-pipeline):
     > Ingest, transform, and persist incoming data — turn raw external
     > signals into queryable structured records that the interactive UI
     > and downstream analytics can rely on. -->

## Boundaries (non-goals)

<!-- HUMAN — Required. What this group is NOT. Bullet list of "Does NOT ..."
     statements. Each non-goal redirects to the group / component that DOES
     handle it.

     Example (data-pipeline):
     - Does NOT serve queries to end users (that's `interactive-ui`).
     - Does NOT make schema decisions (those live in shared schemas at
       the project level). -->

## Children

<!-- HUMAN — Required. Sub-groups and/or components contained in this group.
     Bidirectional ownership: each child declares `parent: {{this-group}}`. -->

| Name | Kind | One-line purpose |
|------|------|------------------|

## Integration

<!-- HUMAN — Required. How the children fit together topologically.
     Who calls what; who depends on what; data flow direction.

     If children don't integrate (no calls, no dependencies between them),
     that's a finding worth noting — declare it explicitly rather than
     leaving the section blank. Children that don't integrate may not
     belong in the same group. -->

## Orchestration

<!-- HUMAN — Required. How the children coordinate at runtime / through
     workflows. Execution ordering; async patterns; sequencing.

     If children don't coordinate, declare "N/A — children operate
     independently of each other." -->

## Shared Contracts

<!-- HUMAN — Optional but recommended. Explicit interfaces (function
     signatures, API endpoints, message formats) used across children.
     This is where the §2 quality property "shared grammar across components"
     gets operationalized at the group level.

     Declare "N/A" if children share no contracts. If many children share
     no contracts, that's a diagnostic finding — the group might be doing
     redundant work, or the children might not actually belong together. -->

## Shared Schemas

<!-- HUMAN — Optional but recommended. Data structures shared between
     children. Declare "N/A" if children share no schemas.

     Examples: a record type passed from one child to another; a config
     shape both children read from; an event schema both children produce
     or consume. -->

## Implementation Notes

<!-- HUMAN — Optional. File locations, constraints, and guidance for the
     group's organization itself (NOT for code, since groups own no code). -->

### Design record

<!-- AUTO — Citation list of design specs and plans about this group's
     structure. One line per artifact. No embedded design content. -->

## Decisions

<!-- APPEND-AUTO — /consolidate harvests new rows from design specs and
     plans about this group. Existing rows are never modified. -->

| ID | Decision | Alternatives Considered | Rationale | Date | Source |
|----|----------|------------------------|-----------|------|--------|
